methods.description: This method describes the process for analyzing toxins in P. allochrona strains using LC-MS/TOF. It involves culturing, lysing, and preparing samples for chromatographic and mass spectrometric analysis to detect domoic acid.

steps.description: Cultures of P. allochrona strains SZN-B495 and SZN-B524 were grown in 1 L Erlenmeyer flasks (20°C, irradiance 70-80 μmol photon m −2 s −1 and 12:12 light:dark regime), harvested at their late exponential growth phase and centrifuged (750 g for 10 min).
steps.searchable_summary: Grow and harvest P. allochrona cultures, then centrifuge.

steps.description: The cell pellet was stored at −18°C until analysis.
Cells were lysed by sonication for 5 min, then added with 500 μl of MeOH/H2O (1:1) mix, and vortexed for 3 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Lyse cells by sonication and mix with MeOH/H2O.

steps.description: Cells were lysed by sonication again for 5 min and centrifuged at 1700 g for 5 min.
The clear supernatant was transferred into a glass test tube, and the pellets were resuspended in 500 μl of MeOH/H2O (1:1) and vortexed for 3 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer supernatant and resuspend pellet in MeOH/H2O.

steps.description: All steps were repeated three times.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat lysis and resuspension steps three times.

steps.description: The supernatant was evaporated and the residue resuspended with 500 μl of MeOH/H2O (1:1).
This solution was centrifuged at 7690 g for 5 min and finally 5 μl were analyzed by LC-MS/TOF.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge solution and analyze by LC-MS/TOF.

methods.description: This method involves the ecological analysis of P. delicatissima-like morphs using co-inertia analysis to explore environmental niches and phylogenetic relationships.

steps.description: Based on the collection dates of more than 250 strains over 10 years and the recurring seasonal patterns observed, P. delicatissima-like morphs occurring in summer and autumn were arbitrarily assigned to P. allochrona, while those recorded in winter and spring were assigned to other P. delicatissima-like species.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign P. delicatissima-like morphs to species based on seasonal patterns.

steps.description: The environmental niches of P. allochrona and the other P. delicatissima-like morphs were explored with the co-inertia analysis Outlying Mean Index (OMI).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform co-inertia analysis using OMI to explore environmental niches.

steps.description: The net evolutionary divergence in ITS between P. allochrona and P. arenysensis was calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate evolutionary divergence in ITS between species.
